description_summary: A collection of swarthy, compact nomad-peoples east of the Sea of Rhûn — restless confederations ever seeking land and plunder and apt to invade their neighbours, often in the Enemy's service. Toward travellers: warlike and frequently hostile, raiders and invaders dangerous on their own ground or at war.

name: Half-orcs
description: Half-orcs are a hideous creation, born of Man and Orc. They are often confused with Uruk-hai, but are a distinct race, small in number but capable and deadly. Their origin is also unclear, although it appears that they were first used by the tainted Wizard Saruman. He still employs them as agents, spies, lieutenants, and special guards. They are particularly effective in Eriador, for Saruman's Half-orcs have Dunlending blood in them and are capable of blending into their societies. Half-orcs are akin to Uruk-hai, but look more like dark Men. They speak Westron quite well (Rank 5), and most know both Orkish (Rank 3) and a little Black Speech (Rank 2). Half-orcs have no restrictions on professions, and get three background points.
description_summary: A hideous crossbreed of Man and orc, first made by the corrupt wizard Saruman — small in number but capable and deadly, and (with Dunlending blood) able to pass among Men as spies and agents. Toward travellers: cunning and malevolent infiltrators who may hide in plain sight before they strike.

name: Uruk-hai
description: Following Sauron's resurrection in T.A. 1000 he began breeding a new race of Orcs, one capable of independent and intelligent action. After centuries of work, he produced the first Greater Orcs, and he called them the Uruk-hai (BS "Orc-race") because they were more suited to the formation of societies. Initially, the Uruk-hai remained close to Sauron and served as lieutenants and elite guards, but gradually their numbers grew and their strain strengthened. Their existence remained guarded until Sauron was willing to show his hand. Nonetheless, in T.A. 2475 they were unleashed in full scale battle formations. They are the Dark Lord's finest goblin troops. The Uruk-hai differ from normal or Lesser Orcs in many ways: (1) they stand 5-6 feet tall; (2) they have a more "human" appearance, despite cat-like eyes, fang-teeth, and black/grey hides; (3) they have longer, stronger, and straighter legs; (4) they are intelligent and cunning; and (5) despite their preference for darkness, they operate freely in daylight. They speak Black Speech (Rank 5), Orkish (Rank 4), and good Westron (Rank 4).
description_summary: The "great orcs" — a larger, stronger, more intelligent orc-race bred by the Dark Lord, able to act on their own and endure daylight, serving as elite soldiers and captains. Toward travellers: disciplined, fearless and deadly warriors, among the worst foes on any road — and they do not flee the sun.

name: Broadbeam Dwarf Patrol
description: A patrol of Broadbeam Dwarves warding the roads and mine-approaches of the Blue Mountains. Grim and heavily armed, they guard the holds of Nan-i-Naugrim against Orcs and trespassers and keep the old ways open between the Dwarf-towns.
description_summary: A patrol of Broadbeam Dwarves warding the roads and mine-approaches of the Blue Mountains — grim and heavily armed, guarding their holds against orcs and trespassers and keeping the old ways open. Toward travellers: stern, watchful guards, correct with honest folk but quick to challenge strangers near their gates.

name: Black Lizards
description: Dark-scaled lizards of rocky, broken country, basking by day and sheltering in crevices. Most are harmless hunters of insects, though larger kinds will take small prey.
description_summary: Dark-scaled lizards of rocky, broken country, basking by day and sheltering in crevices — most harmless insect-hunters, though larger kinds take small prey. Toward travellers: harmless to people, a sign of dry, stony ground.

name: Haradrim
description: This is a collective name for the peoples who occupy the great arid and semi-arid lands south of Harondor and Mordor, the land called Harad. Since this is rough and unblessed territory, their greatest populations lie beside the seacoast, rivers, and bays; however, Harad contains little true desert and sparse groups settle or roam the whole region. Haradrim (S. "Southmen") are also called "Southrons" or "Haradwaith" (a term also referring to the land). Physical Character: North and central desert - Most groups are small and wiry, particularly those of the open country: males average 130 pounds, women 100 pounds. Coastal areas and Far Harad- Most are related to the Men of Mûmakan, and are tall and wiry: males average 180 pounds, women 140 pounds. Northern groups have light/medium grey or brown skin, straight black hair, and dark brown eyes. Southern groups have dark grey, brown, or black skin; straight or curly black hair; and dark brown or black eyes. +10 bonus versus heat/fire attacks; -10 bonus versus cold/ice attacks. Their eyes are attuned to extremely bright light and they are unaffected by brilliant displays which might blind others.
description_summary: The "Southrons" — the many peoples of the hot, arid lands south of Gondor and Mordor, mostly small and wiry, settling along coasts, rivers and bays as herders, farmers, traders and warriors, some swayed to the Enemy's banner. Toward travellers: varied folk, hospitable or hostile by tribe and allegiance, and often suspicious of northerners.

name: Slaves
description: Captives and subjugated people from various backgrounds who work the land to support the warrior elite, although the harsh climate forces some warriors to continue agricultural labor.
description_summary: Captives and subjugated folk of many origins, worked under the lash to support a warrior elite — broken and desperate people. Toward travellers: pitiable more than threatening, but the desperate can turn dangerous, and where they toil their cruel masters are near.

name: Mithrim Noldor Patrol
description: A roving war-band of Noldor of Mithrim descent that keeps the old marches of the Blue Mountains and northern Lindon. They watch the passes of the Ered Luin and the wooded vales below for Orcs out of the ruined North, and escort travellers between the scattered Elven settlements.
description_summary: A roving Noldor war-band of the Blue Mountains and northern Lindon, watching the passes of the Ered Luin for orcs out of the ruined North and escorting travellers between scattered Elven settlements. Toward travellers: vigilant Elven guards — protective of the free and a help to honest wayfarers, sharp toward anything that smells of the Enemy.

name: Lossoth
description: The Lossoth live in the Far North of western Middle-earth and are often called "Snowmen" or "Forodwaith" (the latter is also a name for their land). They are a sparsely settled, nomadic folk who move with the seasonal migrations of big game and rarely interact with other Men. Physical Character: Stocky and hard, with pronounced muscles. They have wide features and large, pudgy hands and feet. Men average 175 pounds, women 140 pounds. Fair, with reddish highlights. They have pale blue eyes and fair hair. They are extremely rugged and can travel great distances with little or no rest. Short; men average 5'5", women 5'3". Lifespan: Medium, about 75-90 years. +20 bonus versus cold/ice attacks. -20 bonus versus heat/fire attacks. They have an acute sense of smell and perception. They can pick up a Man's scent a mile downwind and 100 feet upwind (1000 feet otherwise). +10 bonus to Perception. The Lossoth are poor, nomadic hunters and gatherers. They use stonework, bone, and limited amounts of wood and metal (mostly copper). Some herd reindeer and all make use of hunting dogs. They hate Wargs, Dragons, Giants, and Trolls above all things.
description_summary: The "Snowmen" of the far north — a sparse, nomadic, cold-hardened folk, stocky and tough, who move with the migrations of big game and rarely deal with other Men. Toward travellers: reclusive and shy of outsiders but not hostile; a rare source of aid and lore in the deadly cold.

name: Falathrim (Sea-elves)
description: Sea-elves who dwell by the coasts, skilled in shipbuilding and sailing. Along the shores of Lindon and Harlindon they appear as fishers and net-menders in the river-mouths, as shipwrights and sea-captains in the havens, and as the crews of the grey ships that keep the western coasts.
description_summary: Sea-elves of the western coasts, masters of shipbuilding and sailing — fishers and net-menders in the river-mouths, shipwrights and captains in the havens, crews of the grey ships that ward the shores. Toward travellers: courteous coast-dwelling Elves, bound to sea and ship, reserved with strangers but not unkind.

name: Bramble
description: Dense, thorny bramble and briar that chokes wood-edge, ruin and waste — a tangle that tears clothes and flesh and slows any passage. In some old or tainted places the briars grow rank and clutching, almost as if with a will of their own.
description_summary: Dense, thorny bramble and briar that chokes wood-edge, ruin and waste — a tangle that tears clothes and flesh and slows any passage; in old or tainted places it grows rank and almost clutching, as if with a will of its own. Toward travellers: a painful, slowing barrier — and in dark places, a hedge that seems to grasp at those who push through.

name: Calenardhons Soldiers
description: Soldiers of Calenardhon, the great grassland province of Gondor before it was granted to the Rohirrim — garrison troops and militia holding its forts, fords and roads against Orcs and Dunlending raiders out of the hills.
description_summary: Garrison troops and militia of Calenardhon — Gondor's grassland province before Rohan — holding its forts, fords and roads against orcs and Dunlending raiders out of the hills. Toward travellers: disciplined king's soldiers, allies to the lawful and watchful of strangers on a raided frontier.

name: Urban Men
description: This is a generalized label for the town and city folk of Eriador and Gondor, the Common Men of northwestern Middle-earth. It includes common folk from Annúminas, Fornost, Bree, Tharbad, Calembel, Dol Amroth, Linhir, Pelargir, Osgiliath, Minas Tirith, etc. These folk contain varying degrees of Harnadan, Dunlending, Northman, and Dúnadan blood. Physical Character: All types, but normally medium. Males average 160 pounds, women 125 pounds. Variations of fair to tan or olive skin. All colors of hair and eyes. Average endurance. Males average 5'10", women 5'4". Lifespan: Moderate, averaging 65-85 years. Various types of elaborate or practical wool and linen garb, including cloaks, boots, etc. Tunics are worn in warmer areas; shirts and pants or leggings are worn in cooler locales. Some imported silks and fine cottons are in evidence. Urban folk are a varied, rather cosmopolitan lot. Many are from mercantile or guild backgrounds and some have dealt with enchantments. Urban folk are worldly enough to deal with most outsiders. They hate no race, aside from the Orcs and Wargs.
description_summary: A broad label for the town and city folk of Eriador and Gondor — the common people of places like Bree, Tharbad, Pelargir and Minas Tirith — of mixed blood and every trade. Toward travellers: ordinary townsfolk used to strangers and traffic, their welcome ranging from busy indifference to friendliness or suspicion.

name: Bree-landers
description: Mixed folk of Bree and its environs, including men of various stocks and hobbits, living in and around a trenched, hedged village at the junction of the Greenway and Great East Road, once a vibrant trade-town but fading by T.A. 1640.[file:25]
description_summary: Mixed folk of Bree — Men of various stocks and hobbits together — living in and around a hedged, trench-ringed village at a great crossroads, once a busy trade-town and now somewhat faded. Toward travellers: used to strangers and the road; generally welcoming but cautious, with a homely, gossipy good sense.

name: Dunlending Site
description: A site of the Dunlendings — a hill-fort, clan-hall, standing stone or burial cairn of the old folk of Dunland. Wary of strangers, the clans guard their places jealously and resent trespass.
description_summary: A place of the Dunlendings — a hill-fort, clan-hall, standing stone or burial cairn of the old folk of Dunland. Toward travellers: jealously guarded by wary clans who resent trespass; approach uninvited at your risk.

name: Dorwinrim
description: The Dorwinrim occupy the lower Carnen valley and the region running south from the river to the northwest shore of the Sea of Rhûn - the land called Dorwinion. They are a mixture of Northman and Easterling stock. Physical Character: Dorwinrim are slightly larger than most groups labeled as "Common Men": males average 160 pounds, females 110 pounds. Overall, they are somewhat stocky and have wide features; narrow noses and eye slits are the only exception. Their fair, slightly yellowish complexion is unique. They have subdued eyes which are usually brown (like their straight hair), although some are blue or green. Average endurance. Men average 5'9", women 5'2". Lifespan: 60-80 years. +30 bonus to RR versus poisons. +15 for Acting or Public Speaking situations. +10 for Rowing maneuvers. They are traders and river-men who abandoned their nomadic past in favor of an urban, albeit rustic life. Dorwinion is known for its fine wines and strong oils. Vines cover the land and provide the principal sources of income: grapes, olives, wine, olive oil, etc.
description_summary: The folk of Dorwinion by the Sea of Rhûn — of mixed Northman and Easterling blood, somewhat stocky and fair with a faint yellowish cast — a people of the wine-lands between the great realms. Toward travellers: traders and vintners of the eastern marches whose welcome depends on the dealings and the day.

name: Avalanche
description: A sudden slide of snow or rock down a steep slope, triggered by thaw, storm or a careless noise, that can bury a party in moments. The high passes are most treacherous in spring and after heavy snow.

description_summary: A sudden slide of snow or rock down a steep slope, set off by thaw, storm or even a careless shout, that can bury a party in moments — worst in the high passes in spring and after heavy snow. Toward travellers: a swift, smothering killer of the mountains; move quietly and choose the hour.

name: Asdriag & Sagath Soldiers
description: Warriors of the Asdriag and Sagath, Easterling tribes of the lands beyond Rhûn who serve Sauron's banners as auxiliaries. Horse-borne and lightly armed, they fight for plunder and the promises of dark lords, and trouble the eastern bounds of the free realms.
description_summary: Easterling warriors of the lands beyond Rhûn who ride under Sauron's banners as auxiliaries — horse-borne, lightly armed, fighting for plunder and dark lords' promises. Toward travellers: hostile raiders of the eastern marches, a danger to any of the free peoples they meet.

name: Crocodiles
description: Large water-lurking reptiles of the warm southern rivers, marshes and shores, lying like logs until prey comes near. Powerful and armoured, a big one can drag down a deer, a horse or an unwary man.
description_summary: Large water-lurking reptiles of warm southern rivers and shores, lying like logs until prey comes near — powerful and armoured, a big one able to drag down a deer, a horse or an unwary man. Toward travellers: a deadly ambush at any warm waterside; safe at a distance, lethal within a lunge.

name: Beijabar of Nan Anduin
description: The Beijabar, a Northman folk of the Vales of Anduin akin to the Beornings — bee-keepers, herders and woodsmen who hold the riverlands and fight to keep them clear of Orcs and wolves. Independent, and hospitable to friends but fierce to foes.
description_summary: A Northman folk of the Anduin vales akin to the Beornings — bee-keepers, herders and woodsmen who hold the river-lands and keep them clear of orcs and wolves. Toward travellers: independent folk, hospitable to friends but fierce to foes.

name: Dunedain Traveller
description: A lone Dúnadan upon the road — one of the long-lived, far-wandering Men of Westernesse, abroad on some errand of their own. Grave, courteous and watchful, such a traveller makes a doughty companion and keeps their lineage and purpose close.
description_summary: A lone Dúnadan on the road — one of the long-lived, far-wandering Men of Westernesse, abroad on some errand of their own; grave, courteous and watchful. Toward travellers: a doughty, honourable companion who keeps their lineage and purpose close.

name: Chasm
description: A deep cleft, crevasse or sinkhole — sometimes plain, sometimes hidden under brush or thin crust — into which the unwary may plunge. Old quarries, karst country and glaciers are riddled with such pitfalls.
description_summary: A deep cleft, crevasse or sinkhole — sometimes plain, sometimes hidden under brush or thin crust — into which the unwary may plunge; karst country, old quarries and glaciers are riddled with them. Toward travellers: a silent pitfall; test the ground in broken or hollow country.

name: Druedain Site
description: A place of the Drúedain (Woses) — a carved Púkel-stone, hidden camp or forest shrine, marked by their watchful guardian-statues. Easily overlooked and best left undisturbed by outsiders.
description_summary: A place of the Drúedain — a carved Púkel-stone, hidden camp or forest shrine marked by their watchful guardian-statues. Toward travellers: easily overlooked and best left undisturbed; the Wild-men guard their places, and their statues may be more than stone.

name: Dunedain Site
description: A site of the Dúnedain — a ranger-shelter, boundary-stone, cache or ruined steading of the Men of Westernesse. Plainly made and well-hidden, such places often still serve those who know their secrets.
description_summary: A site of the Dúnedain — a ranger-shelter, boundary-stone, cache or ruined steading of the Men of Westernesse, plainly made and well-hidden. Toward travellers: often still useful to those who know its secrets, a likely refuge for the ranger-wise.

name: Falathrim Fisher Sailor
description: Falathrim of the Lindon coasts met about their daily craft — casting nets in the river-mouths, mending boats on the strand, or working a small smack along the Gulf of Lhun. Weathered and unhurried, they know every current and tide of Harlindon's shores.
description_summary: Sea-elves of the Lindon coast met at their daily craft — casting nets in the river-mouths, mending boats on the strand, working a small smack along the gulf — weathered and unhurried, knowing every current and tide. Toward travellers: calm, courteous coast-folk and a good source of passage and sea-lore for the respectful.

name: Dunlendings Priests
description: Keepers of the old Dunlending faith — leaders of rite and sacrifice who tend the clans' ancestral spirits at hill-shrines and standing stones. They hold great sway over their people and deep suspicion of outland gods and ways.
description_summary: Keepers of the old Dunlending faith — leaders of rite and sacrifice who tend the clans' ancestral spirits at hill-shrines and standing stones, holding great sway and deep suspicion of outland gods and ways. Toward travellers: powerful and insular, hostile to outsiders who meddle with their rites or sacred places.

name: Dunlendings Warriors
description: Fierce clan-warriors of Dunland, armed with spear, axe and round shield, who nurse an old hatred of Rohan for the lands their forefathers lost. They fight in loose bands under their headmen and raid the Westfold when the chance comes.
description_summary: Fierce clan-warriors of Dunland with spear, axe and round shield, nursing an old hatred of Rohan for lost lands, fighting in loose bands under their headmen and raiding the Westfold when they can. Toward travellers: dangerous and quick to violence, above all toward Rohirrim and their friends.

name: Dwarf Prospector
description: A lone Dwarf or small party searching the roots of the Blue Mountains for fresh seams of iron, coal or precious metal. Secretive about their finds, prospectors range the high valleys with pick, lamp and a keen eye for the lie of the stone.
description_summary: A lone Dwarf or small party searching the roots of the Blue Mountains for fresh seams of iron, coal or precious metal — secretive about finds, ranging the high valleys with pick, lamp and a keen eye for stone. Toward travellers: gruff and close-mouthed about their work, but no threat to honest folk.

name: Foreigners
description: Men from distant lands, marked by strange dress, speech and custom — traders, envoys, mercenaries or wanderers far from home, regarded with curiosity or suspicion depending on where their road has brought them.

name: Dyrians
description: Folk of Dyr in the eastern marches near Dorwinion and the Sea of Rhûn — traders, herders and rivermen living between the great realms, prizing their wine, horses and hard-won independence.
description_summary: Folk of Dyr in the eastern marches near the Sea of Rhûn — traders, herders and rivermen living between the great realms, prizing their wine, horses and hard-won independence. Toward travellers: proud, self-reliant border-folk; fair traders, wary of being ruled.

name: Farmer Woodsman
description: Settled country folk who work a farm or a stretch of woodland at the edge of the wild — tilling, herding, felling and charcoal-burning. Hospitable but guarded, they are the first to suffer when raiders or beasts come out of the wilderness.
description_summary: Settled country folk working a farm or stretch of woodland at the edge of the wild — tilling, herding, felling and charcoal-burning; hospitable but guarded, and the first to suffer when raiders or beasts come out of the wilderness. Toward travellers: kindly to honest folk and a source of food and shelter, but wary on an exposed frontier.

name: Edain Ruin
description: A ruin of the ancient Edain or their early kingdoms — older and ruder than the works of Gondor and Arnor, half-sunk in earth and forest. Such places hold old bones, older lore, and sometimes things best left buried.
description_summary: A ruin of the ancient Edain or their early kingdoms — older and ruder than the works of Gondor and Arnor, half-sunk in earth and forest. Toward travellers: it holds old bones, older lore, and sometimes things best left buried — a place to search with care.

name: Dwarf Merchant
description: A Dwarven trader on the road with a laden pony or wagon, dealing in metalwork, tools, weapons and worked goods between the Dwarf-holds and the towns of Men and Elves. Shrewd bargainers, they travel well-guarded and keep their accounts close.
description_summary: A Dwarven trader on the road with a laden pony or wagon, dealing in metalwork, tools and weapons between the Dwarf-holds and the towns of Men and Elves — a shrewd bargainer travelling well-guarded. Toward travellers: open to trade and news, canny and close with their accounts, but fair to honest dealers.

name: Volcanic Hazards
description: The perils of fire-mountains and hot ground — lava, ash, choking fumes, scalding steam-vents and bubbling mud-pots. Found chiefly about Orodruin and other live peaks, where the very earth is treacherous.
description_summary: The perils of fire-mountains and hot ground — lava, ash, choking fumes, scalding steam-vents and bubbling mud — found chiefly about Orodruin and other live peaks where the very earth is treacherous. Toward travellers: a land that can burn, choke or scald; every step near the fire-mountains is a gamble.

name: Elven Site
description: A place of the Elves — a hidden refuge, garden, white tower or carved fountain, often beautiful and lightly guarded by enchantment. Time and the world pass it by, and few mortals find it unbidden.
description_summary: A place of the Elves — a hidden refuge, garden, white tower or carved fountain, often beautiful and lightly guarded by enchantment, that time and the world pass by. Toward travellers: serene and lovely, but seldom found unbidden; the enchantment that hides it may also turn the unwelcome away.

name: Eagles
description: Great hunting eagles of mountain and moor, sharp-eyed takers of hare, fowl and lamb. Larger and prouder than other raptors, they nest among high crags and are seldom troubled by any beast of the lowlands.
description_summary: Great hunting eagles of mountain and moor — sharp-eyed takers of hare, fowl and lamb, larger and prouder than other raptors, nesting among the high crags. Toward travellers: little threat to people, a majestic sight wheeling over the heights.

name: Great Elk
description: An immense elk of the northern wilds, far larger than the common kind, with a towering spread of antlers. Rare and magnificent, a great bull fears few beasts and can gore or trample any that corner it.

name: Eriadorian Site
description: A site of the mingled folk of Eriador — a village, mill, shrine or roadside ruin of the ordinary Men of the lands between the mountains. Humble and well-worn, the stuff of everyday life rather than legend.
description_summary: A site of the mingled folk of Eriador — a village, mill, shrine or roadside ruin of the ordinary Men between the mountains, humble and well-worn. Toward travellers: the stuff of everyday life rather than legend, a likely place for food, rest or simple news.

name: Garth
description: An enclosed yard, garden or homestead-close — a walled plot of orchard, herb-beds or pasture about a dwelling. A small island of order and plenty, and a place a traveller might beg food or shelter.
description_summary: An enclosed yard, garden or homestead-close — a walled plot of orchard, herb-beds or pasture about a dwelling, a small island of order and plenty. Toward travellers: a likely place to beg food or shelter, and a sign of settled, tended land.

name: Lossoth Site
description: A camp or sacred place of the Lossoth, the Snowmen of Forochel — snow-houses, sled-caches and bone-marked shrines on the frozen coast. Suited to the bitter North and unwelcoming to outsiders.
description_summary: A camp or sacred place of the Snowmen of Forochel — snow-houses, sled-caches and bone-marked shrines on the frozen coast, suited to the bitter North. Toward travellers: unwelcoming to outsiders and hard to use without Lossoth knowledge, but a sign that help against the cold may be near.

name: Hobbits
description: A rare meeting with Hobbits beyond their usual haunts — small, soft-spoken folk easily overlooked, generally a family or two strayed from the settled lands of the Shire, Bree or Dunland. Shy of big folk and quick to slip from sight, they want little more than to be left to their meals and pipes.
description_summary: A rare meeting with hobbits beyond their usual haunts — small, soft-spoken folk easily overlooked, usually a family or two strayed from the settled lands. Toward travellers: shy of big folk and quick to slip from sight, wanting little more than to be left to their meals and pipes.

name: Flash Flood
description: A wall of water that roars down a dry wash or gorge after sudden rain upstream, sweeping away anything in the channel with little warning. Deadly in narrow defiles where the sky above may be clear.

Toward travellers: a sudden, drowning danger; never camp in a dry streambed in storm-country.

name: Fisherfolk
description: Folk who live by the water — netting fish in river and lake, gathering shellfish and shrimp along the shores, and poling boats through the shallows. Weathered and close-knit, they keep to their own stretch of bank or coast and read the water better than any map.
description_summary: Folk who live by the water — netting fish, gathering shellfish along the shores, poling boats through the shallows — weathered, close-knit, and reading the water better than any map. Toward travellers: settled waterside folk, helpful for passage and food, keeping to their own stretch of bank or coast.

name: Cultirith Patrol
description: A patrol of soldiery based at the stronghold of Cultirith, warding its district against raiders and brigands, keeping the local roads and challenging strangers passing through their lord's lands.
description_summary: A patrol of soldiery from the stronghold of Cultirith, warding its district against raiders and brigands, keeping the roads and challenging strangers in their lord's lands. Toward travellers: lawful men-at-arms; no threat to the honest, but they will stop and question those who pass.

name: Eels
description: Long, snakelike fish of muddy rivers, meres and estuaries, hunting by night and hiding in weed and silt by day. Slippery and hard to hold, the larger ones bite sharply and some salt-water kinds grow alarmingly big.
description_summary: Long, snakelike fish of muddy rivers, meres and estuaries — night-hunters that hide in weed and silt by day; slippery and hard to hold, the larger ones biting sharply, and some sea-kinds growing alarmingly big. Toward travellers: little real threat — a nuisance to anglers and a startle to waders, save for the rare giant kind.

name: Insect Swarm
description: A dense swarm of biting or stinging insects — flies, midges, gnats or worse — thick enough to torment beast and man, foul wounds and drive cattle to madness. The bane of marsh and still summer air.
description_summary: A dense swarm of biting or stinging insects — flies, midges, gnats or worse — thick enough to torment man and beast, foul wounds and drive cattle mad; the bane of marsh and still summer air. Toward travellers: a maddening, sapping torment that can spoil a march or a camp, especially near stagnant water.

name: Hillmen
description: Rough hill-dwelling folk of the upland country — herders, hunters and raiders who answer to clan and feud rather than king. Hardy and clannish, wary of lowlanders and quick to take up arms, they hold the high valleys and moors that softer folk avoid.
description_summary: Rough hill-dwelling folk of the uplands — herders, hunters and raiders who answer to clan and feud rather than king, hardy and clannish, holding the high valleys softer folk avoid. Toward travellers: wary of lowlanders and quick to arms; their welcome depends on clan and circumstance.

name: Glacier
description: A great river of ice, slow-moving and riddled with hidden crevasses, unstable séracs and meltwater channels. Crossing one is a peril in itself, and the bitter cold and glare add to the danger.
description_summary: A great slow river of ice riddled with hidden crevasses, unstable ice-towers and meltwater channels — a peril to cross, with bitter cold and blinding glare adding to the danger. Toward travellers: treacherous footing where a hidden crevasse can swallow a careless step; not to be crossed lightly.

name: Tribesmen
description: Folk of a clan or tribe living beyond the settled realms — hillmen, marsh-dwellers, herders or hunters bound by kin and custom rather than king. Proud and wary of outsiders, and fierce in defense of their own ground.
description_summary: Folk of a clan or tribe beyond the settled realms — hillmen, marsh-dwellers, herders or hunters bound by kin and custom rather than king. Toward travellers: proud and wary of outsiders, peaceable if respected but fierce in defence of their own ground.

name: Giant Vampire Bat
description: An oversized blood-drinking bat of the southern and eastern lands that descends on sleeping beasts and men to feed. Its bite is scarcely felt until the creature is gorged, and a colony can leave livestock weak and bloodless.

Toward travellers: a stealthy night-feeder on the sleeping — post a watch where they haunt.

name: Fell beasts of the Emyn Guldur
description: The winged fell beasts that roost in the Emyn Guldur near Dol Guldur — featherless, reptilian flyers bred or drawn to the Necromancer's service and later borne by the Nazgûl. Their shadow and reek bring terror before ever they strike.
description_summary: Featherless, reptilian winged monsters that roost in the hills near Dol Guldur, bred or drawn to the Necromancer's service and later borne by the Nazgûl; their shadow and reek bring terror before they strike. Toward travellers: a horror of the sky near the dark hill — dread goes before them, and they hunt at evil command.

name: Gondor Garrison Patrol
description: Soldiers of a Gondorian garrison out on their rounds — keeping the walls, roads and watch-posts of a town or fortress, checking travellers and watching the borders. Disciplined men of the South Kingdom, courteous to the lawful and hard on the suspect.
description_summary: Soldiers of a Gondorian garrison on their rounds — keeping the walls, roads and watch-posts, checking travellers and watching the borders; disciplined men of the South Kingdom. Toward travellers: courteous to the lawful and hard on the suspect; they will stop and question strangers.

name: Great Lizard
description: A large predatory lizard of the southern wilds, big enough to threaten dogs, livestock and children. Quick over short distances, it ambushes prey and defends its lair with snapping jaws and lashing tail.
description_summary: A large predatory lizard of the southern wilds, big enough to threaten dogs, livestock and children — quick over short bursts, ambushing prey and defending its lair with snapping jaws and lashing tail. Toward travellers: a real danger to the small and the careless near its lair, and dangerous if cornered.

name: Grass Fire
description: A wildfire racing across dry grassland or scrub, wind-driven and faster than a running horse. It can trap herds and travellers in the open, and the smoke alone can choke or blind.
description_summary: A wildfire racing across dry grassland or scrub, wind-driven and faster than a galloping horse, that can trap herds and travellers in the open — and the smoke alone can choke or blind. Toward travellers: a swift, deadly danger of dry plains; flee crosswind, never ahead of it.

name: Great Serpent
description: A huge serpent of the southern marshes and rivers, long enough to coil and crush a man or a large beast. Patient and hidden, it strikes from the water's edge and drags its prey under.
description_summary: A huge serpent of southern marshes and rivers, long enough to coil and crush a man or a large beast — patient and hidden, striking from the water's edge and dragging prey under. Toward travellers: a lethal ambush at warm waterside; it crushes and drowns whatever it seizes.

name: Guard Tower
description: A fortified tower set to watch a road, ford, border or pass — manned in good times, ruinous or ill-held in bad. From its top a long stretch of country can be watched, and travellers below are seldom unseen.
description_summary: A fortified tower set to watch a road, ford, border or pass — manned in good times, ruinous or ill-held in bad; from its top a long stretch of country is watched. Toward travellers: shelter and lawful aid if friendly-held, a trap or a watching eye if it has fallen — and you are rarely unseen beneath it.

name: Lynx
description: A medium-sized, tuft-eared wildcat of the northern forests, hunting hares, birds and small deer. Solitary and elusive, it keeps to dense cover and is more often heard than seen.
description_summary: A medium-sized, tuft-eared wildcat of the northern forests, hunting hares, birds and small deer — solitary, elusive and keeping to dense cover, more often heard than seen. Toward travellers: shy and no threat to people, a fleeting shadow among the trees.

name: Horsefolk Éored
description: A mounted troop of the Éothraim horse-folk — riders ranging the grass-plains in war or on patrol, swift and disciplined, deadly with spear and bow from the saddle. Whether a small scouting party or a full éored, they cover ground no footman can match.

name: Greater Dragon
description: A dragon of the first rank — ancient, immense and terrible, often fire-breathing and wing-borne, with a guileful tongue and a hoard to match its age. Few remain, and the coming of one is a calamity for kingdoms, not merely travellers.
description_summary: A dragon of the first rank — ancient, immense and terrible, often fire-breathing and wing-borne, with a guileful tongue and a hoard to match its years; few remain, and the coming of one is a calamity for whole kingdoms. Toward travellers: not a danger but a doom — to face one is to face the ruin of realms.

name: Hobbit Wanderer
description: A lone Hobbit far from home, an oddity among his kind driven by restlessness, exile or some half-mad errand of the sort that gets a Hobbit 'talked about.' Footsore and out of place yet surprisingly hardy, such wanderers turn up in the strangest corners of the world.
description_summary: A lone hobbit far from home — an oddity among his kind, driven by restlessness, exile or some half-mad errand; footsore and out of place yet surprisingly hardy, turning up in the strangest corners of the world. Toward travellers: harmless and out of his depth, but good company and tougher than he looks.

name: Laegrim Ranger
description: A ranger of the Laegrim, the Green-elves of the woods, warding the eaves of the forest realms. Silent woodsmen and deadly archers, they patrol the wild marches, shadow intruders and keep the old paths between the Elven lands.
description_summary: A ranger of the Green-elves warding the eaves of the forest realms — a silent woodsman and deadly archer who patrols the wild marches, shadows intruders and keeps the old paths. Toward travellers: unseen until he chooses; a guardian who watches strangers closely and aids the worthy.

name: Hunting Lizard
description: A swift, pack-hunting lizard of the warm lands, running down small game in coordinated rushes. Bold and persistent, a pack can pull down prey far larger than any single one of them.
description_summary: A swift, pack-hunting lizard of the warm lands, running down small game in coordinated rushes — bold and persistent, a pack able to pull down prey far larger than any one of them. Toward travellers: a single one is minor, but a pack is dangerous and dogged.

name: Lions
description: Great tawny cats of the warmer plains and scrublands of the South, hunting deer, wild cattle and the herds of Men. They hunt by ambush and stealth, and a hungry pride will not shrink from a lone traveller.
description_summary: Great tawny cats of the warm southern plains and scrub, hunting deer, wild cattle and the herds of Men by ambush and stealth — and a hungry pride will not shrink from a lone traveller. Toward travellers: a serious danger in their country, above all to the solitary and after dark.

name: Woodland birds of Rhosgobel
description: The many woodland birds that gather about Rhosgobel, home of Radagast the Brown, who befriends and speaks with them. Quick to carry word through the forest, they serve as eyes and messengers for the bird-tamer of the Wood.
description_summary: The many woodland birds that gather about Radagast's home, befriended by the bird-tamer — quick to carry word through the forest and serving as his eyes and messengers. Toward travellers: harmless and watchful; through them, friendly word may reach the Brown Wizard.

name: Merchants & Traders
description: Traders plying their goods between towns and lands — cloth, metalwork, salt, wine and small luxuries, with a laden pony, a pack or a guarded wagon. Shrewd and talkative, they carry news as readily as wares and know the roads and prices of half a kingdom.
description_summary: Traders plying goods between towns and lands — cloth, metalwork, salt, wine and small luxuries — with a laden pony, pack or guarded wagon; shrewd, talkative, carrying news as readily as wares. Toward travellers: welcoming and useful for goods and news, canny in a bargain but no threat.

name: Minas Ithil Courier
description: A rider bearing messages to and from Minas Ithil — once a fair tower of Gondor, later the dread Minas Morgul. In the realm's good days an honest courier of the South Kingdom; in its evil days, a servant of the Nazgûl carrying word no honest man should read.
description_summary: A rider bearing messages to and from Minas Ithil — an honest courier of Gondor in the realm's good days, but a servant of the Nazgûl carrying foul word once the tower became Minas Morgul. Toward travellers: in good times a lawful messenger; in evil times something to avoid — its errand is dark.

name: Nobles & Leaders
description: A person of rank and authority abroad with their retinue — a lord, lady, captain or chieftain on some errand of state, war or kinship. Such folk travel attended and armed, expect deference, and can prove a powerful friend or a perilous enemy.

name: Mountain Goat
description: A wild goat of the high crags, agile and sure-footed on cliffs that would daunt any predator. The rams bear heavy curved horns, and the herds keep to heights where few hunters can follow.
description_summary: A wild goat of the high crags, agile and sure-footed where no predator can follow, the rams bearing heavy curved horns and the herds keeping to the heights. Toward travellers: harmless; a sure-footed sight on cliffs far above the road.

name: Dwarves
description: A chance meeting with Dwarves abroad — usually a small party on the road between their mountain-holds and the markets of Men, rather than a delving in their halls. Blunt, watchful and quick to take offence or oath, they keep their own counsel and rarely tarry.

name: Scout Elves
description: Elven scouts ranging the wild country about Rivendell and the Trollshaws, watching the fords and old roads of Rhudaur. They move unseen, mark the passage of Orcs and brigands, and carry word swiftly back to the Last Homely House.
description_summary: Elven scouts ranging the wild country about Rivendell and the Trollshaws, watching the fords and old roads — moving unseen, marking the passage of orcs and brigands and carrying word swiftly home. Toward travellers: hidden guardians who observe strangers before showing themselves, and help the worthy in peril.

name: Ooze
description: Treacherous soft ground — quicksand, quagmire, sucking mud or slime-filled pools — that grips and swallows whatever blunders in. The more a victim struggles, the deeper they sink.
description_summary: Treacherous soft ground — quicksand, quagmire, sucking mud or slime-filled pools — that grips and swallows whatever blunders in, the deeper for every struggle. Toward travellers: a silent trap of marsh and bog; test the footing, and do not thrash if caught.

name: Orc Females
description: Female Orcs, seldom seen abroad and kept mostly within the deep warrens where the breed is reared. Met in the tunnels of Moria and like strongholds, they are as vicious as the males in defense of their broods.
description_summary: Female orcs, seldom seen abroad and kept mostly in the deep warrens where the breed is reared — met in the tunnels of Moria and like strongholds, as vicious as the males in defence of their broods. Toward travellers: hostile and savage in their lairs, encountered only deep in orc-holds and no less deadly there.

name: Orc Caravan
description: A train of Orcs hauling supplies, plunder or slaves across the Enemy's lands, plodding under whip and guard between Nurn's fields and the towers of Mordor. Slow and ill-tempered, it is heavily watched but vulnerable if caught in the open.
description_summary: A train of orcs hauling supplies, plunder or slaves across the Enemy's lands, plodding under whip and guard between Nurn's fields and the towers of Mordor — slow, ill-tempered, heavily watched but vulnerable in the open. Toward travellers: hostile and well-guarded; a thing to avoid or ambush, not to meet head-on.

name: Sailors
description: Seafaring and river-going men met in port or aboard ship — crewing trade-vessels, fishing boats and ferries. Hardy, superstitious and free with a tale, they know the coasts, currents and harbours of their waters.
description_summary: Seafaring and river-going men met in port or aboard ship — crewing trade-vessels, fishing boats and ferries; hardy, superstitious and free with a tale, knowing the coasts, currents and harbours. Toward travellers: easy company and a source of passage and sea-lore, harmless ashore unless badly crossed.

name: Pirates
description: Sea-raiders who prey on coastal shipping and settlements, putting in to plunder and slipping away with their spoil. Lawless and dangerous, they range the western and southern coasts, sometimes in league with the Corsairs of Umbar, sometimes for themselves alone.
description_summary: Sea-raiders who prey on coastal shipping and settlements, putting in to plunder and slipping away with their spoil — lawless and dangerous, sometimes in league with the Corsairs, sometimes for themselves. Toward travellers: a direct danger by sea and shore; they rob, take captives and kill.

name: Orc Warband
description: A war-band of Orcs on the march or raid — dozens of fighters with their captains, sometimes mounted or trailing a baggage-train. Bound for plunder, slaughter or muster, such a band is a serious threat to any small company that crosses its path.

name: Orc Trackers
description: Orcs trained to hunt by scent and sign, set on the trail of escaped thralls, spies or travellers. Tenacious and savage, the elite among them can follow a cold trail for days and rarely give up a quarry once found.
description_summary: Orcs trained to hunt by scent and sign, set on the trail of escaped thralls, spies or travellers — tenacious and savage, the best able to follow a cold trail for days and rarely giving up a quarry. Toward travellers: a relentless pursuer; once they have your scent they are hard to shake.

name: Brigands & Outlaws
description: Lawless men of the road and the wild — robbers, deserters, broken soldiers and hired ruffians who prey on travellers and isolated farms. They band together where order is weak, demand toll or plunder at knife-point, and scatter before any real strength of arms.

name: Orc Warg-riders
description: Orcs mounted on Wargs — the great evil wolves of the North — fast, far-ranging and deadly. Used as raiders, outriders and shock troops, they strike at speed across open country and harry fugitives and caravans before melting back into the wild.
description_summary: Orcs mounted on the great evil wolves of the North — fast, far-ranging raiders and shock-troops that strike at speed across open country and harry fugitives and caravans before vanishing. Toward travellers: a swift, deadly threat in the open; they run down the slow and the lone.

name: People Fur Trappers
description: Men who range the northern woods and wilds trapping beasts for their pelts — solitary, hardy and weather-worn, coming to town only to sell furs and lay in supplies before vanishing back into the trees.
description_summary: Men who range the northern woods trapping beasts for pelts — solitary, hardy and weather-worn, coming to town only to sell furs and resupply before vanishing back into the trees. Toward travellers: gruff loners and no threat, a possible source of guidance and trade in the deep wilds.

name: People Hunters
description: Men who live by the chase, hunting game in forest, hill and fen for meat and hides. Skilled with bow and snare and at home in the wild, they range far from any settlement and read sign that townsfolk would never notice.
description_summary: Men who live by the chase — hunting game in forest, hill and fen for meat and hides, skilled with bow and snare and at home far from any settlement, reading sign townsfolk would miss. Toward travellers: self-reliant woodsfolk, helpful and knowledgeable, wary but rarely hostile.

name: People Reed Peat Cutters
description: Marsh-folk who cut reed for thatch and dig peat for fuel from the bogs and fens, working the wetlands by flat-bottomed boat. Poor and little regarded, they know the safe paths through mires that swallow the unwary.
description_summary: Marsh-folk who cut reed for thatch and dig peat for fuel, working the bogs by flat-bottomed boat — poor and little regarded, but knowing the safe paths through mires that swallow the unwary. Toward travellers: humble fen-folk and a precious guide through treacherous marsh for those who treat them kindly.

name: Severe Weather
description: Killing weather — blizzards, ice-storms, hail, gales and freezing rain — that can fell the unsheltered traveller through cold and exhaustion alone. The high country and far North are deadliest when the sky turns.
description_summary: Killing weather — blizzards, ice-storms, hail, gales and freezing rain — that can fell the unsheltered through cold and exhaustion alone, deadliest in the high country and far North when the sky turns. Toward travellers: a deadly test of shelter and endurance; seek cover before it closes in.

name: Elves
description: A chance meeting with Elves abroad in the wider lands — usually a small party on the road rather than the folk of a settled realm: travellers between the havens, a wandering company keeping to the dusk, or hunters far afield. Courteous but reserved with strangers, they seldom linger long.

name: Merchant Caravan
description: A train of merchants travelling together for safety — wagons, pack-beasts and hired guards strung along the trade-roads between the great markets. A rich and tempting prize for brigands, and so rarely met without an escort of armed men.
probability_by_region: [{"region": "Central Anduin", "probability": 1}, {"region": "Central Misty Mountains", "probability": 1}, {"region": "Nan Anduin", "probability": 2}, {"region": "North Misty Mountains", "probability": 1}, {"region": "Rohan", "probability": 2}, {"region": "Ronen-in-Anduin", "probability": 1}, {"region": "Westmarch", "probability": 1}, {"region": "Wold", "probability": 2}, {"region": "Nurn", "probability": 3}]
description_summary: A train of merchants travelling together for safety — wagons, pack-beasts and hired guards strung along the trade-roads; a rich prize for brigands, and so rarely seen without an armed escort. Toward travellers: welcoming and a source of goods and news, well-guarded and wary of ambush.

name: Pilgrim Wanderer
description: A lone traveller on a journey of faith or penance, bound for some shrine, holy place or vow far from home. Footsore and single-minded, pilgrims trust to the charity of those they meet and to the protection their purpose is thought to grant.
description_summary: A lone traveller on a journey of faith or penance, bound for some shrine or vow far from home — footsore and single-minded, trusting to charity and to the protection their purpose is thought to grant. Toward travellers: harmless and devout, glad of kindness and company and no threat.

name: Pilgrim to Mithlond
description: An Elf making the last journey west, bound for the Grey Havens to take ship into the Undying Lands. Such pilgrims pass quietly through Lindon, alone or in small companies, their thoughts already half upon the sea and the long weariness of the Ages plain upon them.
description_summary: An Elf making the last journey west to the Grey Havens to take ship into the Undying Lands — passing quietly through Lindon, alone or in small companies, thoughts already on the sea and the weariness of ages plain upon them. Toward travellers: gentle and sorrowful, wholly turned westward; courteous but already half-departed from the world.

name: Plague Refugee
description: Folk fleeing pestilence — driven from a stricken town or village, carrying what little they could and perhaps the sickness itself. Desperate and shunned, refugees beg shelter and food, and the wary turn them away for fear of what they bring.
description_summary: Folk fleeing pestilence — driven from a stricken town, carrying what little they could and perhaps the sickness itself; desperate and shunned, begging shelter and food. Toward travellers: pitiable but a real danger — the wary turn them away for fear of the plague they may carry.

name: Renegade Elf
description: An Elf who has forsaken his people — outlawed for some old deed, soured by grief, or gone solitary and strange in the wilder corners of Lindon. Wary and unpredictable, a renegade may aid a traveller as readily as rob one, and answers to no lord.
description_summary: An Elf who has forsaken his people — outlawed for some old deed, soured by grief, or gone solitary and strange in the wild — wary, unpredictable, and answering to no lord. Toward travellers: a wild card who may aid a stranger as readily as rob one, and not to be trusted on sight.

name: Poisonous Fumes
description: Foul or deadly vapours — marsh-gas, volcanic fumes or the airs of some tainted place — that sicken, stupefy or kill those who breathe them. Often unseen, they pool in hollows, caves and stagnant low ground.
description_summary: Foul or deadly vapours — marsh-gas, volcanic fumes or the airs of some tainted place — that sicken, stupefy or kill, often unseen and pooling in hollows, caves and stagnant low ground. Toward travellers: an invisible killer; suspect bad air in caves and hollows, and do not linger where the ground stinks.

name: Sindar Guardian
description: A Sindarin warden set to keep some threshold of the Elven lands — a ford, a forest-eaves, a hidden path. Silent and watchful, clad to melt into wood and shadow, the guardian challenges intruders and speeds friends quietly on their way.
description_summary: A Sindarin warden set to keep some threshold of the Elven lands — a ford, a forest-eaves, a hidden path — silent and watchful, clad to melt into wood and shadow. Toward travellers: challenges intruders from hiding but speeds friends quietly on their way; a guardian, not a hunter.

name: Sea Captain Sailor
description: An Elven ship-master of Cirdan's folk, commanding one of the grey vessels that ply the Gulf of Lhun and the coasts of Harlindon. Long-sighted and sea-hardened, such captains carry messages, goods and passengers between the havens — and, at the last, those bound west.
description_summary: An Elven ship-master of Círdan's folk, commanding one of the grey vessels of the Gulf of Lhûn and the Harlindon coasts — long-sighted and sea-hardened, carrying messages, goods and passengers between the havens, and at the last those bound west. Toward travellers: courteous and capable, a means of passage and sea-lore for the worthy.

name: Scouts
description: Lightly-armed men ranging ahead of a larger force or watching a stretch of country — outriders, foresters and trackers in someone's service. They keep out of sight, mark the movements of friend and foe, and carry word swiftly back.
description_summary: Lightly-armed men ranging ahead of a larger force or watching a stretch of country — outriders, foresters and trackers in someone's service, keeping out of sight and carrying word swiftly back. Toward travellers: watchful and elusive; whether they help or hinder depends on whom they serve.

name: Rivermen
description: Men who live and work along the great rivers — boatmen, ferriers, fishers and traders who move goods and folk by water. They know every shoal, landing and backwater, and keep a shrewd independence from the lords of the dry land.
description_summary: Men who live and work the great rivers — boatmen, ferriers, fishers and traders moving goods and folk by water, knowing every shoal and landing and keeping a shrewd independence from the land's lords. Toward travellers: useful for passage and river-lore, independent-minded but generally fair to honest custom.

name: Sand glass storm
description: A blinding storm of wind-driven sand and grit that scours skin, chokes the lungs and erases the trail, common in the southern deserts. It can rob a caravan of its bearings and bury landmarks in hours.
description_summary: A blinding storm of wind-driven sand and grit that scours skin, chokes the lungs and erases the trail, common in the southern deserts — robbing a caravan of its bearings and burying landmarks in hours. Toward travellers: a disorienting, choking peril of the desert; cover the face, halt, and wait it out.

name: Slaves and Guards
description: A coffle of slaves driven under armed guard — captives bound for the markets or the labor of some master, herded along the road by hard-eyed keepers. The guards are watchful and brutal; the slaves, broken but always alert for a chance to flee.
description_summary: A coffle of captives driven under armed guard toward the markets or some master's labour, herded by hard-eyed keepers — the guards watchful and brutal, the slaves broken but alert for any chance to flee. Toward travellers: the guards are violent and suspicious; the slaves may beg aid or seize a chance to break free.

name: Southerners
description: Men of the southern lands — Haradrim and their neighbours, dark-haired and richly or strangely arrayed — met as traders, envoys, mercenaries or soldiers far north of their homes, and often eyed with mistrust in the lands of the West.
description_summary: Men of the southern lands — Haradrim and their neighbours, dark-haired and richly or strangely arrayed — met as traders, envoys, mercenaries or soldiers far north of home, often eyed with mistrust in the West. Toward travellers: their intent varies by errand and allegiance; treated with suspicion in northern lands, friend or foe by circumstance.

name: Snow Leopard
description: A solitary, grey-dappled cat of the high cold peaks, superbly suited to crag and snow. A patient stalker of goats and sheep among the heights, shy of Men and rarely seen in the lowlands.
description_summary: A solitary, grey-dappled cat of the high cold peaks, superbly suited to crag and snow — a patient stalker of goats and sheep among the heights, shy of Men and rarely seen low. Toward travellers: shuns people; a danger only to one who surprises it or strays into its high hunting-ground.

name: Serganka
description: A rare bird of the eastern wilds, prized and little understood, met only in remote country. Wary and striking in plumage, it keeps far from the haunts of Men.
description_summary: A rare, striking-plumaged bird of the eastern wilds, prized and little understood, met only in remote country and keeping far from the haunts of Men. Toward travellers: harmless and elusive, a wondrous fleeting sight in far-off lands.

name: Spiders
description: The many spiders of wood, cave and ruin, most small and harmless but some grown large and venomous in dark, tainted places. Their webs choke old passages and forest hollows, and the bigger kinds take birds and small beasts.
description_summary: The many spiders of wood, cave and ruin — most small and harmless, but some grown large and venomous in dark, tainted places, their webs choking old passages and forest hollows. Toward travellers: mostly no threat, but the larger kinds in dark places can bite and bind, and their webs foul the way.

name: Thieves & Smugglers
description: Footpads, cutpurses and smugglers who work the towns, fairs and trade-roads, moving stolen goods and contraband, slipping past tolls and gates, and lightening the purses of the unwary. Quick-fingered and quicker to vanish, they answer to whoever pays.

name: Snow Lions
description: Pale, thick-furred great cats of the northern mountains and snow-fields, well camouflaged against ice and stone. Powerful ambush hunters of goats, deer and elk, they are seldom seen until they strike.
description_summary: Pale, thick-furred great cats of the northern mountains and snow-fields, camouflaged against ice and stone — powerful ambush hunters of goats, deer and elk, seldom seen until they strike. Toward travellers: a stealthy, dangerous predator of the cold heights, rarely seen but deadly when it springs.

name: Spies
description: Agents abroad on someone's secret business — watching roads and garrisons, carrying word, or worming into the confidence of the powerful. They pass as travellers, merchants or servants, and a careless one is as dangerous to its master as to its mark.

name: Toads
description: Frogs, toads and other amphibians of pond, marsh and damp wood, loud in the breeding season and harmless to all but insects. Some marsh-toads carry an acrid skin-poison that sickens any beast foolish enough to mouth them.
description_summary: Frogs, toads and other amphibians of pond, marsh and damp wood — loud in the breeding season and harmless to all but insects, though some marsh-toads carry an acrid skin-poison that sickens any beast that mouths them. Toward travellers: harmless, a danger only to a dog or beast foolish enough to bite one.

name: Wandering Company
description: A band of folk travelling the roads together — players, pilgrims, displaced families or companions on a long road, sharing fire and protection in numbers. Their fortunes and friendliness vary, but a company on the move always carries news from afar.

description_summary: A band of folk travelling the roads together — players, pilgrims, displaced families or companions on a long road — sharing fire and the safety of numbers. Toward travellers: friendliness and fortunes vary, but a company on the move always carries news from afar and welcomes company on a lonely stretch.

name: War Wolves
description: Huge dogs (S. Ormaraka, aka Yelmaraka), often mistaken for Wargs, bred and trained for combat and frequently fielded in numbers as trackers, scouts or to harass enemy armies. The War-wolves of Waw serve as combat units, while those of Angmar are used as mounts by Orcish raiders and Orc-lords. They look like merely overlarge Wolves, which is true, but they are bolder, far more violent and much more cunning.
description_summary: Huge war-dogs, often mistaken for Wargs but truly only overlarge wolves bred and trained for battle — bolder, fiercer and more cunning than wild wolves, fielded in numbers as trackers and harriers and even ridden by orcs in the North. Toward travellers: trained killers loosed in packs, a serious danger where the Enemy or war-bands field them.

name: Wild Hobbits
description: Hobbits living rough and un-Shirelike at the edges of settled country — in the eaves of the Old Forest, the Bree-land hedges and the lonely downs. Warier and more weather-hardened than their kin, they trust no big folk and vanish into bracken and burrow at the first sign of trouble.
description_summary: Hobbits living rough at the edges of settled country — in the eaves of old forests, hedges and lonely downs — warier and more weather-hardened than their kin, trusting no big folk and vanishing into bracken at the first alarm. Toward travellers: shy and elusive and no threat, gone from sight before you know they were there.

name: Wild Dogs
description: Feral dogs running in packs through the wilds and the fringes of settlement, scavenging carrion and pulling down small or weakened prey. Cowardly alone but bold in numbers, they are a persistent nuisance to herders and travellers.

name: Lisicavi
description: Small, large-eared desert fox (Ap. Nightears, pl. Lisicavi) found in Harad, thriving in the cool of the desert night. It hunts in pairs or small family groups for rodents and large insects and digs out sleeping reptiles. It is hunted for its brilliant auburn coat.
description_summary: A small, large-eared desert fox of Harad, abroad in the cool of the night, hunting in pairs or family groups for rodents and large insects and digging out sleeping reptiles; hunted for its bright auburn coat. Toward travellers: harmless and shy, a quick big-eared shape slipping through the desert dusk.

name: Golden Eagle
description: A large golden-brown eagle of the high country, a powerful hunter of hares, marmots and the young of mountain herds. It soars on broad wings over crag and valley and defends a wide hunting range.

name: Kirinkir
description: The Kirinki is a tiny scarlet bird with an extremely high chirp that was one of the most beloved songbirds of Numenor. After the Downfall in S.A. 3319, knowledge of these wondrous singers became confined to verse, illustrations in books, and works of art depicting the little creatures. Some of the Faithful who escaped from Numenor, however, brought their pet Kirinkir with them. Whether any survived or thrived is another matter, although there have been rumors that Radagast the Brown has a pair at his home at Rhosgobel (near the southwestern eaves of Mirkwood).
description_summary: A tiny scarlet songbird with an extremely high, sweet chirp — one of the most beloved birds of lost Númenor, now all but vanished and known mostly from old verse and art, though a few may yet survive (rumour gives Radagast a pair). Toward travellers: harmless and wondrous, a vanishingly rare jewel of a bird and a marvel to glimpse.

name: Rárorassi
description: The largest and most powerful of Endor's Sheep, the Rútorasse (Q. "Eastern Woolhorns"; pl. "Rútorassi") is a stout, black and silver beast that stands about 4-5 feet at the shoulder. It is aptly named, for its silvery head fur grows up the sides of its curved horns. These two forward-facing weapons can be lethal, and the Woolhorn is quick to employ them for defense; thus, this beast is difficult to domesticate. Native to the lands of Dalpygis, Chey, and the Kargagis Ahar in central Middle-earth, Woolhorns roam at night in small, segregated herds. Males run apart from the females except during the mating season, which lasts from late summer until early fall. When united, the mixed herd is relatively stationary for about a month, the first half of which is devoted to courting rituals, including brutal duels between males seeking dominance (and their choice of mates).
description_summary: The largest and most powerful of sheep — a stout black-and-silver beast standing four to five feet at the shoulder, its silvery head-fur running up its curved, forward-facing horns, which it is quick to use and which make it hard to tame. Toward travellers: a wild herd-beast, harmless if left alone but dangerous with those lethal horns if pressed.

description: Owls of wood, ruin and barn, hunting silently by night for mice, voles and small birds. Their calls carry far in the dark, and country folk read luck or ill omen in them; by day they roost hidden in hollow trees and rafters.

name: Nut Groves
description: Groves and wild stands harvested for nuts — beech-mast, hazel and walnut — gathered in autumn for food, oil and trade. The mast also fattens the pigs turned loose to forage in the woods.
description_summary: Groves and wild stands harvested for nuts — beech-mast, hazel and walnut — gathered in autumn for food, oil and trade, the mast also fattening the pigs loosed to forage. Toward travellers: a welcome source of food in season, and a sign of woods worked by nearby folk.

name: Herb-lands
description: Meadows, woods and tended gardens worked for herbs — for medicine, cooking, dye and craft. Herb-wives know the right seasons and places, and healing simples from such lands are valued far beyond the region.
description_summary: Meadows, woods and tended gardens worked for herbs — for medicine, cooking, dye and craft — whose healing simples are valued far beyond the region. Toward travellers: a source of remedies and food for those who know the plants, and a sign of herb-wise folk nearby.

name: Mines & Diggings
description: Workings cut into hill and mountain for metal and stone — iron, tin, copper, lead, silver, gems or mithril, hewn in shaft and gallery. Spoil-heaps, smelters and guarded ore-roads mark the diggings, and the deeper delvings can wake older perils.
